title: Thesis / Dissertation Outline
note: A chapter structure for an engineering/technology thesis on the OmniDrone concept, mapped to the repository files that supply each chapter. Written for a DESIGN-AND-FEASIBILITY thesis (which is what TRL 3 supports), with a clear path to a results-bearing thesis once validation data exists.

the_honest_framing.what_kind_of_thesis_this_can_be_now: A design, specification, and feasibility study with a pre-registered validation methodology. It contributes a problem analysis, a system architecture, an identified research gap, and a testable plan. It does NOT contribute empirical results, because none exist.
the_honest_framing.what_it_becomes_after_validation: An empirical thesis once Phase 2-3 data exists — the profile-vs-reference agreement, the calibration-uncertainty curve, and the stratification-precursor analysis are all publishable results.
the_honest_framing.the_discipline: Write in proposal/design tense now. Do not use results language until there are results. A committee will forgive an honest feasibility study; it will fail a study that claims validation it does not have.
the_honest_framing.the_tempting_error: Presenting the synthetic sample data as results. The illustrative_synthetic data exists ONLY to build the webpage. It must never appear in a results chapter. See sample-telemetry.json.

chapters.title: Introduction
contents: Background: the aquaculture inflection and its significance
The reframe: monitoring as a labour-economics problem
Scope and delimitation (what the system does NOT do)
chapters.note: Use the problem taxonomy's coverage classification as the scope delimitation — it's already structured as inclusion/exclusion criteria.

chapters.title: Review of Related Literature and Studies
contents: The five-move argument (aquaculture context → failure mechanism → monitoring gap → remote-sensing calibration problem → digital-twin observation famine)
Thematic synthesis across the eight study collections
chapters.the_advice: Lead with the Laguna de Bay triad (DO + ammonia + H2S) — it's a published, mechanistic, Philippine finding that independently justifies the vertical-profiling design. Don't bury your best evidence. AND: verify every citation before submission — the bibliography currently has ZERO opened sources.
chapters.critical_warning: The bibliography is a scaffold, not a finished reference list. Every source must be retrieved and confirmed before this chapter is submitted. See references-bibliography.json#/CRITICAL_WARNING_READ_BEFORE_CITING.

chapters.title: System Design and Specification
contents: Hardware architecture (airframe, winch, sensors, dispensing, power, dock)
Software architecture (pipeline, fusion, models, twin, telemetry, API)
The core architectural contribution: self-calibrating observation
Design trade-offs and the mass-budget finding
chapters.note: The mass-budget analysis (which does NOT close fully-configured) is a genuine engineering finding and belongs here honestly, not hidden. It demonstrates rigorous feasibility analysis — a strength, not a weakness.

chapters.title: Methodology
contents: The control-group problem and its ethical resolution
Analysis plan (Bland-Altman for agreement, calibration-uncertainty curves, mixed-effects for chronic loss)
chapters.note: This chapter is unusually strong for a feasibility thesis because the methodology is pre-registered. The control-group ethical analysis and the explicit falsification conditions are genuine scholarly contributions. Foreground them.

chapters.title: Results
chapters.current_state: EMPTY. No results exist. In the feasibility version of the thesis, this chapter is replaced by 'Feasibility Analysis' — the payload budget, the energy budget, and the risk analysis, which ARE completable now and constitute an analytical feasibility result.
chapters.the_hard_rule: Do NOT populate this with synthetic sample data. Ever.

chapters.title: Discussion
contents: The system's contribution to the identified gap
Limitations (weather, benthic blind spot, affordability, unvalidated prediction)
The ethical tensions (over-stocking, antimicrobial use, equity, surveillance)
chapters.note: The limitations and ethical-tensions sections are where an honest thesis distinguishes itself. Include the over-stocking paradox and the affordability barrier as first-class discussion points, not buried caveats.

chapters.title: Conclusion and Recommendations
contents: Answers to the research questions (to the extent the feasibility study can answer them)
Recommendations for the next development phase

front_and_back_matter.abstract: See elevator-pitches.json#/the_thesis_abstract_version — written in honest proposal tense.
front_and_back_matter.references: references-bibliography.json — with the standing warning that every entry must be verified before submission.

what_this_thesis_genuinely_contributes: A structured problem analysis grounding a documented, recurring, mechanistically-understood failure mode in published evidence.
A reframing of the monitoring problem as labour-economics rather than instrumentation.
A system architecture whose central novelty is self-generated calibration ground truth, addressing a gap the UAV water-quality literature repeatedly identifies.
A feasibility analysis (mass, energy, risk) that honestly reports where the concept does and does not close.
A pre-registered validation methodology including an ethical resolution to the control-group problem and explicit falsification conditions.
the_contribution_statement.what_it_does_not_claim: Empirical validation of any performance or predictive capability. That is future work, and the thesis says so plainly.
the_contribution_statement.why_this_is_a_legitimate_thesis: A design-and-feasibility study with a rigorous problem analysis, an identified gap, a specified system, and a pre-registered method is a complete and defensible scholarly contribution at this stage. It becomes a stronger, results-bearing thesis after the validation season — but it does not need to overclaim to be worth defending now.
